title: rubus glandulosocalycinus
intro: Rubus glandulosocalycinus, also known as the Himalayan raspberry or Himalayan bramble, is a species of flowering plant in the rose family that is native to the Himalayan region. The plant has been traditionally used in medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antioxidant properties. The health benefits of Rubus glandulosocalycinus include reducing inflammation and pain, improving digestion, and boosting the immune system. The plant is often used in herbal preparations such as teas, tinctures, and infusions, and can be found in some traditional medicine systems, including Ayurveda and Unani medicine.
